How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

About Tapal

Tapal is Pakistan's most-loved tea brand, crafting bold, full-bodied blends for the perfect desi cup since 1947. Each batch is blended to deliver a consistent, full-bodied cup that pairs perfectly with warm milk and aromatic spices — the hallmark of a great masala chai.

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

Description:
PG Tips Original Black Tea Bags (300 Count, 870g) (870 g) is an authentic tapal tea — a bold, full-bodied desi black tea — crafted for South Asian tea lovers in Sweden. Recreate the genuine taste of Indian-style milk tea and masala chai in your own kitchen.

About Tapal

Tapal is Pakistan's most-loved tea brand, crafting bold, full-bodied blends for the perfect desi cup since 1947. Each batch is blended to deliver a consistent, full-bodied cup that pairs perfectly with warm milk and aromatic spices — the hallmark of a great masala chai.

Perfect for

  • doodh patti
  • masala chai
  • karak chai
  • iced chai

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

  • milk tea

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

Description:
TRS Mango Pulp Kesar 850g (850 g) is an authentic indian tea — a authentic Indian-style black tea — crafted for South Asian tea lovers in Sweden. Recreate the genuine taste of Indian-style milk tea and masala chai in your own kitchen.

Perfect for

  • masala chai
  • milk tea
  • Indian-style chai
  • morning tea

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

  • morning tea

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

Fruiti-O Juice Drink 2.1L - Refreshing Tropical Flavours (2.1 kg) is an authentic indian tea — a authentic Indian-style black tea — crafted for South Asian tea lovers in Sweden. Recreate the genuine taste of Indian-style milk tea and masala chai in your own kitchen.

  • masala chai
  • milk tea
  • Indian-style chai
  • morning tea

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

Tata Premium Tea 1kg (1 kg) is an authentic indian tea — a authentic Indian-style black tea — crafted for South Asian tea lovers in Sweden. Recreate the genuine taste of Indian-style milk tea and masala chai in your own kitchen.

Perfect for

  • masala chai
  • milk tea
  • Indian-style chai
  • morning tea

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

How to brew the perfect chai

Add one teaspoon per cup to cold water, bring to a boil, then add an equal part of full-fat milk. Simmer for 2–3 minutes, sweeten to taste, and strain. For masala chai, add freshly crushed cardamom, ginger, and a clove at the boiling stage.

Why Indian chai is different

Traditional masala chai uses a CTC (cut, tear, curl) black tea that releases bold colour and flavour quickly when simmered with milk — very different from a European teabag steeped in hot water.

Description:

Serving tip

Why South Asian pickles and chutneys?

In Indian and Pakistani cuisine, pickles (achar) and chutneys are not just condiments — they are an integral part of the meal. A spoonful of mango pickle alongside plain dal and rice transforms it into a complete, satisfying dish. These bold flavours are hard to replicate and even harder to find in regular Swedish supermarkets.

Storage

Refrigerate after opening. Always use a clean, dry spoon. Oil-based pickles keep for up to 6 months; water-based chutneys for 2–4 weeks once opened.
